summary: This fragment describes the procedural application of class actions in the context of Civil Procedure.
key_points: Procedural rules govern how class actions is applied in practice.
Federal Rules establish the framework for implementation.
Courts follow specific steps when addressing this issue.
Parties must meet certain burdens or requirements to invoke this procedure.
Timeline and filing requirements vary by jurisdiction and case type.
Appellate review standards apply to procedural determinations.

philosophy_guard.statement: This fragment describes what IS - the existing legal framework, procedures, and analytical perspectives. It does not advocate for what OUGHT to be.
